mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0%, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding residential mortgage debt indicate strong consumer credit expansion and wealth effects, which jointly stimulate aggregate demand and lift equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, or SPX 60-day returns are flat when both HOUST and RSAFS rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index signals recovering manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns during early economic recoveries.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth correlates negatively with SPX 5-day returns across three consecutive recessions, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 3.0 percent while the federal funds rate remains elevated, real interest rates turn deeply negative, triggering capital flight from equities and compressing forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 55% of triggered episodes, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression spanning 10 years.

mechanism_narrative: Continuous quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that translates directly into higher corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with SPX 60-day forward returns over a 15-year sample, or the signal produces negative returns during confirmed expansionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, which sustains consumer demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns in early-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal generates negative SPX 5-day returns in more than 50% of confirmed early-cycle episodes, or the interaction effect vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts exceeding 5 percent over three months signals weakening consumer confidence and construction activity, which reduces aggregate demand and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered housing contraction episodes, or the signal fails to predict market declines during the 2007-2009 financial crisis.
